target: 9. Another Ending
Hizuru Minakata
August 20
Today marks my farewell to Hitogashima.

(The days since then felt
both long and short…)
(At the very least, it's certain that my life has completely changed.)
(Since then, I haven't heard the straw man's voice. I wonder if he returned to the future?)
You're leaving, huh?
Nezu…
Stop calling me without an honorific. No matter how many times I say it, you just don't get it.
Don't mind it.
I do mind!

Hmph… well, okay.
Where are you going?
We're moving to Tokyo. My father apparently has some connections there.

Tokyo, huh?
It's so far away.
My parents said... it's too painful for them to stay on this island anymore.

There's nothing you can do about it.
What about you, though?
Aren't you the same?

I don't have such feelings myself.
Rather…
I don't want to leave Ryunosuke on this island. That's more like it.
It seems you still can't believe that story.

There's nothing to be done.
It's such an absurd story.

You're right.
It's common to say that the deceased
live on in one's heart…
But for a dead person to actually be inside your body, not as a metaphor or anything... it's hard to believe right away, isn't it.
But I know full well from seeing it myself that impossible things exist on this island.
I see...

I can't leave that monster unchecked.
I'll keep hunting that monster on this island.
Even against a monster, you have a hunter's mindset.

I already know how to defeat it.
How could I let something like that get the better of me?

Eventually, I'll figure out their habits,
effective attacks,
and even their weapons.
That is, if I don't kick the bucket early on.
I see... But I don't think you'll encounter them very frequently from now on.
Why do you say that?
There's a high chance they've been on this island since a long time ago.

Yet they've remained unknown to most,
so they've probably been avoiding attention
and refraining from flashy activities.
That's why they probably rarely attack just anyone indiscriminately.
This island is small. Even one person going missing is a huge deal. It would immediately be known as an incident.
That's exactly why the monsters haven't acted openly. And that goes for Haine too.
That's why she avoided people. And that's why she was starving for them.
I see…
The monsters you'll be facing, Nezu, are more troublesome than starving carnivores. Be careful.
Don't worry, I won't do anything reckless like you did.
I'll send you a letter when I have free time. Letters are better than emails or phone calls, right?

If you feel like it, you can also tell me
about your hunting results via letter.
What're you gonna do knowing that?
They're an opponent who caused all that. It's not strange to want to at least gather information on them, is it?
Hmph... Well, if I feel like it.
Yeah, that helps. About that day, about Haine... There's still way too much I don't understand.

I should go now.
My parents are waiting.
Don't ever come back, you hear?
Yeah, if this story lets me.
You're spouting nonsense again.
Well then, this is goodbye.
Thank you for saving me that day. I'm sure I'll continue to be grateful for our meeting for the rest of my life.
...Hmph, when you say it, it sounds like there's some ulterior motive.
How rude. Well, it's easier for me if you just take it like that, though.

Then, this time for real,
it's goodbye.
Yeah, take care.
(Asako came to see me off, and she's been waving her hand while crying...)
(Nezu is... already gone, huh.)
Hitogashima... It's getting smaller and smaller.
(From here on, I'll have to carve out my own future.)

(And the path I walk from now on
will one day…)
(connect back to that island, I suppose.)
(Yes... Regardless of whether I want it or not.)

Goodbye, Haine.
Next time we meet…
It'd be nice if we could have a fair and square fight.
